title: Add native LCI location injection endpoint
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-zte2.4 added the shared app-interface-to-HAL bridge for local-client location samples, but the gateway local-client CoAP surface still only accepts text/status inbound resources. What needs to be done: add a no-hardware-testable local-client location POST path beside gateway_inbound_text_post() and gateway_inbound_status_post(), parse the compact app location payload into lichen_app_location_time_snapshot, call lichen_app_location_submit_to_hal(), and verify the submitted fix through lichen_hal_location_time_snapshot_get().
notes:
Implementation evidence 2026-07-02:
- Added compact /inbound/location POST parser in lichen/apps/gateway/src/inbound_coap.c and public gateway_inbound_location_post() declaration. Payload format: version 1, flags, fix_state, reserved=0, required lat/lon i32 e7, optional altitude i32, fix_time_unix u32, satellites u8, horizontal/vertical accuracy u32 mm, age_seconds u32. Parser rejects unknown flags, bad version/reserved byte, truncation/trailing bytes, invalid fix state/coordinates, and non-local sources.
- Handler submits accepted samples through lichen_app_location_submit_to_hal() with source_class LOCAL_CLIENT and returns CoAP status from bridge result.
- Security scope: production native BLE and Meshtastic builds do not register /inbound/location in this slice. Registration is limited to ztest-only CONFIG_LICHEN_INBOUND_LOCATION_TEST_SURFACE and CONFIG_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_GATEWAY_ADAPTER_TEST_SURFACE. Production BLE exposure is deferred to child project-LICHEN-zte2.6.1 because native BLE RX currently uses unauthenticated BT_GATT_PERM_WRITE.
- Added focused lichen/tests/inbound_lci_location native_sim/qemu test covering resource registration, scoped BLE-link-local acceptance, unscoped link-local rejection, global source rejection, and HAL snapshot mutation. Added Meshtastic adapter direct parser/HAL tests for all-fields, minimal, sparse optionals, malformed payloads, and forbidden nonlocal source.

Review evidence:
- Initial review Huygens: clean before final auth hardening, verified NET_L2_DUMMY, no Meshtastic production location registration, and scoped native LCI coverage.
- Initial reviews Archimedes and Carson: P1 found native BLE /inbound/location auth bypass if gated only by asserted CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_BLE_AUTHENTICATED_WRITES while ble_uart RX remained BT_GATT_PERM_WRITE; Carson also P2 found test fidelity issue from forced CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_BLE compiler define.
- Fix: removed LORA_LICHEN_BLE_AUTHENTICATED_WRITES Kconfig gate and production registration path, kept /inbound/location resource ztest-only, and removed forced CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_BLE compiler define from inbound_lci_location test.
- Follow-up reviews Averroes and Lagrange: clean, no P0/P1/P2. Both verified /inbound/location registration only under ztest surfaces depending on ZTEST, direct handler source gate intact, and focused test now uses Kconfig test surface without misleading CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_BLE define.
- git diff --check: pass.

EC2/Twister evidence:
- Builder i-0b37b2545da40d11f in us-east-2a with EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 mounted at /mnt/lichen-zephyr. Initial launch could not use file:///mnt/lichen-zephyr/scripts/aws-builder-user-data.sh from local host because the EBS mount is only available after attach, so Ubuntu builder was launched and host packages were bootstrapped after mounting/sourcing the prepared EBS cache.
- Command run in /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-zte2-6-validate: west twister -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_gateway_adapter -T lichen/tests/inbound_lci_location -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --outdir twister-out-zte2-6g --inline-logs --extra-args ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen
- Result: 4 scenarios / 8 instances selected; 4 configs passed, 4 static skipped, 0 failed, 0 errored, 0 warnings in 95.46s; 80 test cases executed, 80 skipped on 2 of total 12 platforms.
- size twister-out-zte2-6g: native_sim meshtastic_gateway_adapter text=253767 data=7235 bss=75024 dec=336026; native_sim inbound_lci_location text=146475 data=8015 bss=31951 dec=186441; qemu_x86 inbound_lci_location text=162748 data=9354 bss=124716 dec=296818; qemu_x86 meshtastic_gateway_adapter text=216012 data=5352 bss=154768 dec=376132.
- Config gates from twister-out-zte2-6g: Meshtastic native has CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_BLE=y and both test surfaces unset; inbound_lci_location native/qemu have CONFIG_LICHEN_INBOUND_LOCATION_TEST_SURFACE=y and CONFIG_NET_L2_DUMMY=y; Meshtastic qemu has CONFIG_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_GATEWAY_ADAPTER_TEST_SURFACE=y and CONFIG_LICHEN_INBOUND_LOCATION_TEST_SURFACE unset.
- Earlier failed attempts documented during development: twister-out-zte2-6 through 6d exposed test expectation/Kconfig/BT userchan/NET_L2_DUMMY issues; twister-out-zte2-6e-inbound passed focused inbound test; final rerun is twister-out-zte2-6g.

Follow-up:
- Created child project-LICHEN-zte2.6.1 to enable production native BLE LCI location writes only after authenticated encrypted GATT permissions/policy and physical BLE smoke evidence.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware scope: implemented compact /inbound/location parser and HAL bridge, added native_sim/qemu ztests for scoped local-client source gating and parser/HAL behavior, recorded EC2 Twister pass and review evidence. Production BLE registration remains intentionally deferred to child project-LICHEN-zte2.6.1 pending authenticated encrypted GATT write policy and physical BLE smoke validation.
